brief: Convert a legacy Wix 'Localshoring vs Offshore' page whose centerpiece is a draggable before/after image comparison slider into blocks. The surrounding prose and CTA map cleanly, but the slider itself has no block.

mustReproduce: Drag to compare an offshore handoff against a local team in the room

missingBlock.name: image-comparison-slider
missingBlock.reason: The page's centerpiece is a draggable before/after comparison: two images stacked with a handle the visitor drags to reveal one over the other. No existing block renders a stateful, client-side drag-to-reveal interaction. `gallery` shows images side by side or in a carousel and `two-column` places two figures next to each other, but neither provides the overlay-and-drag behavior the source depends on. The surrounding prose (`content`) and CTA (`contact-cta`) convert cleanly; only the slider is a real capability gap, so it routes to the block-curation loop (FR-011) for a new `image-comparison-slider` block rather than hand-coding a one-off page.
